seed_rule_set.description: Generic forecast trajectory rubric. It evaluates whether a ReAct forecasting agent understands a resolvable event, retrieves reliable evidence, reasons faithfully, updates probabilities, and uses tools efficiently.

dimensions.name: Question understanding and resolvability
dimensions.description: Whether the agent identifies event subject, time, place, conditions, and settlement criteria.
dimensions.score_5: Clearly extracts event subject, deadline, options, settlement rule, ambiguity, and edge cases.
dimensions.score_3: Understands the broad event but misses some boundary or settlement details.
dimensions.score_1: Treats an open-ended or non-resolvable prompt as directly forecastable.

dimensions.name: Information retrieval quality
dimensions.description: Whether retrieval covers multiple relevant, independent, fresh, and documented sources.
dimensions.score_5: Uses multiple independent, relevant sources and records search/fetch paths.
dimensions.score_3: Uses relevant sources but lacks diversity, freshness, or trace detail.
dimensions.score_1: Uses sparse, irrelevant, duplicate, or undocumented retrieval.

dimensions.name: Source credibility and news quality
dimensions.description: Whether evidence comes from authoritative, transparent, accurate, and independent sources.
dimensions.score_5: Prioritizes primary/official/high-quality sources and distinguishes source independence.
dimensions.score_3: Uses generally credible secondary sources but limited primary verification.
dimensions.score_1: Relies on low-quality, unclear, promotional, or duplicated sources.

dimensions.name: Reasoning chain quality
dimensions.description: Whether the agent links evidence to forecast-relevant variables and uncertainty.
dimensions.score_5: Separates facts, assumptions, counter-evidence, causal links, and uncertainty clearly.
dimensions.score_3: Provides plausible reasoning but weak conflict handling or causal explanation.
dimensions.score_1: Stacks news snippets without auditable evidence-to-forecast rationale.

dimensions.name: Probability update quality
dimensions.description: Whether probabilities are updated from evidence with calibrated magnitude and residual uncertainty.
dimensions.score_5: Shows evidence-driven probability movement, reasonable magnitude, and remaining uncertainty.
dimensions.score_3: Probability is connected to evidence but update magnitude is underspecified.
dimensions.score_1: Probability appears arbitrary or jumps to extremes from weak evidence.

dimensions.name: Tool use and execution efficiency
dimensions.description: Whether the agent uses necessary tools without wasteful or irrelevant steps.
dimensions.score_5: Uses appropriate search/fetch/reasoning steps with complete ReAct trajectories and controlled cost.
dimensions.score_3: Uses the right tools but has some redundant or shallow steps.
dimensions.score_1: Misses necessary tools or performs mostly irrelevant tool calls.

dimensions.description: Whether the agent identifies event subject, time, place, conditions, and settlement criteria. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Clearly extracts event subject, deadline, options, settlement rule, ambiguity, and edge cases.
dimensions.score_3: Understands the broad event but misses some boundary or settlement details.
dimensions.score_1: Treats an open-ended or non-resolvable prompt as directly forecastable.

dimensions.description: Whether retrieval covers multiple relevant, independent, fresh, and documented sources. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Uses multiple independent, relevant sources and records search/fetch paths.
dimensions.score_3: Uses relevant sources but lacks diversity, freshness, or trace detail.
dimensions.score_1: Uses sparse, irrelevant, duplicate, or undocumented retrieval.

dimensions.description: Whether evidence comes from authoritative, transparent, accurate, and independent sources. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Prioritizes primary/official/high-quality sources and distinguishes source independence.
dimensions.score_3: Uses generally credible secondary sources but limited primary verification.
dimensions.score_1: Relies on low-quality, unclear, promotional, or duplicated sources.

dimensions.name: Reasoning chain quality
dimensions.description: Whether the agent links evidence to forecast-relevant variables and uncertainty. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Separates facts, assumptions, counter-evidence, causal links, and uncertainty clearly.
dimensions.score_3: Provides plausible reasoning but weak conflict handling or causal explanation.
dimensions.score_1: Stacks news snippets without auditable evidence-to-forecast rationale.

dimensions.name: Probability update quality
dimensions.description: Whether probabilities are updated from evidence with calibrated magnitude and residual uncertainty. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Shows evidence-driven probability movement, reasonable magnitude, and remaining uncertainty.
dimensions.score_3: Probability is connected to evidence but update magnitude is underspecified.
dimensions.score_1: Probability appears arbitrary or jumps to extremes from weak evidence.

dimensions.name: Tool use and execution efficiency
dimensions.description: Whether the agent uses necessary tools without wasteful or irrelevant steps. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Uses appropriate search/fetch/reasoning steps with complete ReAct trajectories and controlled cost.
dimensions.score_3: Uses the right tools but has some redundant or shallow steps.
dimensions.score_1: Misses necessary tools or performs mostly irrelevant tool calls.

dimensions.name: Official institution sourcing
dimensions.description: Whether the trajectory traces claims to election bodies, courts, agencies, legislation, or official schedules. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Uses primary institutions or direct legal/procedural records.
dimensions.score_3: Uses credible media summaries but limited primary institutional evidence.
dimensions.score_1: Relies on commentary or campaign claims without institutional verification.

dimensions.name: Statement versus execution distinction
dimensions.description: Whether the agent separates rhetoric, pledges, media narratives, legal acts, and executed government decisions. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Clearly distinguishes public statements from binding actions or official execution.
dimensions.score_3: Mentions the distinction but inconsistently applies it.
dimensions.score_1: Treats rhetoric or headlines as resolved political action.

dimensions.name: Procedural and timing feasibility
dimensions.description: Whether the trajectory checks deadlines, legal thresholds, vote counts, procedural calendars, and remaining feasible actions. Domain adaptation target: politics_governance.
dimensions.score_5: Uses concrete procedural constraints and timing feasibility in probability reasoning.
dimensions.score_3: Mentions timing or procedure but lacks quantitative/procedural grounding.
dimensions.score_1: Ignores legal/procedural feasibility.

rounds.feedback: round_1_candidate_1 is currently best. Validation score=2.0, corr(resolved probability)=1.0, corr(Brier)=-1.0. Improve dimensions that better distinguish credible evidence, cutoff compliance, and probability calibration.

dimension_scores.rationale: The trajectory has a complete ReAct record and explicit probability output.
Retrieval found relevant evidence but diversity or depth is limited.
Evidence mostly uses real URLs and includes direct evidence.
The trajectory combines direct evidence, opposing evidence, and complete ReAct reasoning.
Probability output exists but evidence support is limited.
Some sub-agent/tool structure is present but incomplete.
Generic dimension lacks enough matching evidence.
Some sub-agent/tool structure is present but incomplete.
Generic dimension lacks enough matching evidence.
The trajectory lacks enough structure to verify problem understanding.
Sources are mostly synthetic, missing, or unverifiable.
Reasoning is weakly connected to evidence or incomplete.
No clear probability output was found.
Tool use is missing or poorly recorded.
Generic dimension lacks enough matching evidence.
Tool use is missing or poorly recorded.
Generic dimension lacks enough matching evidence.
